subject: Biology
title: Plants and Soil
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Plants and Soil.

hook.text: Soil is not just dirt. It's a living community of minerals, microbes, fungi, and water.

buildup.text: Plants absorb nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium from soil—the three key nutrients for growth.

discovery.text: Mycorrhizal fungi extend root networks by a hundred times, trading minerals for plant sugars.

twist.text: Industrial farming depletes soil. A third of the world's topsoil is degraded.

climax.text: Healthy soil stores carbon, filters water, and supports food production for 8 billion people.

punchline.text: Take care of the soil. Everything grows from it.

quiz.question: What do mycorrhizal fungi trade with plant roots?
